title: StoreHeadMonitorReport
description: A monitor's signed finding that a host's externally-anchored store-head history is (or is not) faithful (chp-v0.2.md section 12, proposal 0023). For each store-head-anchor (sequence N, root R), the monitor reconstructs the head as-of N from the live store and checks it still equals R; a mismatch is a provable rewrite. The report is offline-verifiable: recompute the header and check the monitor's ed25519 signature. A 'forked' verdict carries a divergence block naming the sequence where the live store stopped reproducing the anchored root.

host_id.description: The monitored host whose anchor history was checked.

verified_through_sequence.description: The highest anchor sequence confirmed faithful (for 'forked', the last good sequence before the divergence).

anchor_count.description: How many anchors were in the walked history.

verdict.description: 'consistent': every anchored root still reconstructs from the live store. 'forked': an anchored root no longer reconstructs (a rewrite).

divergence.description: Present ONLY when verdict is 'forked' (omit-when-consistent, the section 10 byte rule). Names where the live store stopped reproducing the anchored root.

sequence.description: The anchor sequence at which reconstruction diverged.

anchored_root.description: The immutable externally-anchored store head at that sequence.

reconstructed_root.description: What the live store now reconstructs for that sequence (differs → rewrite).

canonicalization.description: The canonicalization scheme for the signed header (e.g. chp-stable-v1).

monitor.description: The monitor's identity: the signed report lives with the monitor, not the monitored host.
